changedText: Under Article Text – Table: CPT/HCPCS Codes and CPT/HCPCS Codes Group 1: Codes added 70471. For 70471, under Article Text – Table: Supervising Physician Qualifications added Board Certified* Radiologist or Neurologist with (ASN or UCNS) and under Article Text – Table: Technician Qualifications added General Radiographer, Medical Physicist or ARRT: R.T.-CT. This revision is due to the 2026 Annual/Q1 CPT/HCPCS Code Update and is retroactive effective for dates of service on or after 1/1/26.

entries.contractorName: Palmetto GBA (MAC - Part A, MAC - Part B)
entries.summary: High-signal revision from CMS notes: Under CMS National Coverage Policy added the following regulation: CMS Internet-Only Manual, Pub. 100-02, Medicare Benefit Policy Manual , Chapter 15, §50.4.4.2 Immunizations. Under Article Text formatting changes wer...
entries.narrative: A54767 looks likely to affect coding tables or claim edit logic. Start by checking code sets, charge capture, and any payer-specific routing around this document.
changedText: Under CMS National Coverage Policy added the following regulation: CMS Internet-Only Manual, Pub. 100-02, Medicare Benefit Policy Manual , Chapter 15, §50.4.4.2 Immunizations. Under Article Text formatting changes were made, and the following verbiage was added under the Tetanus section: “ICD-10 Z23 should not be utilized for injury encounters; it is only applicable to routine preventive immunizations. In the absence of an injury or direct exposure to tetanus, preventative immunization is not covered by Medicare A/B.” and “If the provider knows the service is non-covered or is not a Medicare benefit, the GY modifier must be appended to the CPT/HCPCS code indicating the item or service is statutorily non-covered or does not meet the definition of any Medicare benefit.” This revision is retroactive effective for dates of service on or after 11/17/23.
entries.highlight: Under CMS National Coverage Policy added the following regulation: CMS Internet-Only Manual, Pub. 100-02, Medicare Benefit Policy Manual , Chapter 15, §50.4.4.2 Immunizations. Under Article Text formatting changes wer...

changedText: Revision Effective: 03/01/2026 Revision Explanation: - Under Coding Guidance : “A qualified physician for this service is one that has training and expertise that must have been acquired within the framework of an accredited residency and/or fellowship program in the applicable specialty/subspecialty (i.e., Radiation Oncology or Dermatology) OR a dermatologist that has didactic and clinical experience in radiation treatment.” Under General Documentation Requirements: “Documentation of qualifications when requested of training and expertise that must have been acquired within the framework of an accredited residency and/or fellowship program in the applicable specialty/subspecialty (i.e., Radiation Oncology or Dermatology) OR documentation that the dermatologist has didactic and clinical experience in radiation treatment.”
